page_title: "filter Function - utility-tools"
description: |-
  Filter a map or object by value
---

# `filter` Function

Filter a map or object by value

Returns entries from the input map or object where attributes match any of the given condition objects. Within a single condition object all attributes must match (AND). Multiple condition objects are OR'd together. Nested condition values are matched as subsets: an object condition value matches if all its attributes are present in the target.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
filter(input dynamic, condition dynamic...) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The map or object to filter.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `condition` (Variadic, Dynamic, Nullable) One or more condition objects. An entry is kept if it matches any one condition object (OR). Within each object all key=value pairs must match (AND). Object values are matched as subsets, enabling nested attribute filtering.

## Example Usage

### Filter by attribute condition

Pass one or more condition objects to keep entries where all attributes in **any one** condition match.

- **AND within a condition**: `{ env = "dev", public = true }` requires both attributes to match.
- **OR across conditions**: `{ env = "dev" }, { env = "prod" }` keeps entries matching either.

```terraform
locals {
  services = {
    "dev/api"  = { env = "dev",  port = 8080, public = false }
    "dev/web"  = { env = "dev",  port = 443,  public = true  }
    "prod/api" = { env = "prod", port = 8080, public = false }
    "prod/web" = { env = "prod", port = 443,  public = true  }
  }
}

# AND: both env=dev AND public=true must match
output "public_dev_services" {
  value = provider::util::filter(local.services, { env = "dev", public = true })
}
# Result: { "dev/web" = {...} }

# OR: env=dev OR env=prod
output "dev_or_prod" {
  value = provider::util::filter(local.services, { env = "dev" }, { env = "prod" })
}
# Result: all four entries

# OR of non-overlapping sets
output "private_dev_or_public_prod" {
  value = provider::util::filter(local.services, { env = "dev", public = false }, { env = "prod", public = true })
}
# Result: { "dev/api" = {...}, "prod/web" = {...} }
```

### Nested attribute conditions

Condition values that are objects are matched as subsets, allowing filtering on nested attributes.

```terraform
locals {
  all = provider::util::combine([
    { key = "env",     items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region",  items = ["europe-west1", "us-central1"] },
    { key = "service", items = {
      api    = { replicas = 2, tls = false }
      web    = { replicas = 3, tls = true  }
      worker = { replicas = 1, tls = false }
    }},
  ])
}

# Keep only prod entries where the service has tls=true.
# The service object is matched as a subset: { tls = true } matches any service object
# that contains tls = true, regardless of other attributes.
output "prod_tls_services" {
  value = provider::util::filter(local.all, { env = "prod", service = { tls = true } })
}
# Result:
# {
#   "prod/europe-west1/web" = { env = "prod", region = "europe-west1", service = { id = "web", replicas = 3, tls = true } }
#   "prod/us-central1/web"  = { env = "prod", region = "us-central1",  service = { id = "web", replicas = 3, tls = true } }
# }
```

### Using filter with for_each

A common pattern is to use `combine` to generate all combinations, then `filter` to select a relevant subset for a particular resource.

```terraform
locals {
  all = provider::util::combine([
    { key = "env",    items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region", items = ["europe-west1", "us-central1"] },
  ])
}

# Only create prod resources in the european region
resource "google_compute_network" "prod_eu" {
  for_each = provider::util::filter(local.all, { env = "prod", region = "europe-west1" })
  name     = "vpc-${each.value.env}-${each.value.region}"
}
```
